name: The First Central National Bank Of St. Paris
description_short: The First Central National Bank of St. Paris is a local community bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, and personal loans with a focus on hometown service and relationship banking.
description_long:
The First Central National Bank of St. Paris operates as a traditional community bank serving the St. Paris, Ohio area. Based on their NMLS# 514553 and website presence, they function as a full-service financial institution rooted in local banking relationships. The bank maintains a physical presence with a main office at 103 S. Springfield Street in St. Paris and emphasizes personalized service and customer accessibility.

Their product offerings include personal and business checking accounts (with $50 minimum opening deposit for savings), savings accounts, certificates of deposit (currently offering 3.35% APY on 12-month CDs at 3.30% rate), mortgage loans, and a variety of personal loans. Loan categories specifically include auto financing, home renovations, boat loans, camper financing, agriculture loans, and debt consolidation—with the bank noting they "keep and service all of our loans" rather than selling them off. They also mention student accounts at reduced rates for education-focused borrowers.

The bank differentiates itself through its commitment to local service, hometown lending practices, and direct loan servicing. Their marketing emphasizes personal relationships with loan officers and accessibility for mortgage approval. The website positions them as an alternative to larger national banks through community-focused messaging like "The American dream can now become your reality" with hometown service.

A significant caveat: At the time of this profile, the bank's website prominently displays multiple notices of service disruptions affecting online banking due to internet provider outages, with no resolution timeline provided. Additionally, operational hours show limited availability (closed Thursdays at main office, partial hours on other days), which may limit accessibility for some customers. The bank appears stable and legitimate but should be evaluated as a local option rather than a comprehensive digital banking alternative. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CD) with promotional rates
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinance
Agriculture loans for cash flow and inventory
pros: Low minimum deposit requirement ($50) to open savings accounts, making banking accessible
Keeps and services all loans in-house rather than selling them, maintaining customer relationships
Diverse loan product offerings including agriculture, auto, boat, camper, home renovation, and debt consolidation
Competitive CD rates (3.35% APY on 12-month special) for savings customers
Personalized mortgage approval process with dedicated loan officers
Student-focused accounts at reduced rates for education borrowers
Physical branch locations with drive-thru access for in-person banking needs
cons: Online banking service currently disrupted with internet provider issues and no clear resolution timeline
Limited branch hours with main office closed Thursdays and extended hours only on Friday afternoon
Geographically limited to St. Paris, Ohio area, reducing convenience for customers outside the region
Website content gaps make it difficult to compare specific rates for checking, savings, auto loans, and mortgages
No mention of digital banking features, mobile app, or online account opening capabilities

contact.address: 103 S Springfield St, St. Paris, OH 43072

best_for: Local St. Paris area residents and businesses seeking relationship-based community banking
Agricultural borrowers needing specialized lending for farming operations and inventory
Rural customers seeking auto, boat, and recreational vehicle financing with local service
diagnosis: Best suited for St. Paris-area residents and local business owners seeking traditional community banking with personal loan servicing and relationship-focused lending. Main caveat: active online banking service disruptions and limited geographic reach make this unsuitable for remote banking or customers outside Ohio, and digital banking capabilities appear minimal or unavailable.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 103 S Springfield St, St. Paris, OH 43072
